content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 307 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901         322-4441

December 13, 1984


Hilda Cantu Montoy
Deputy City Attorney
City of Fresno
City Hall
Fresno, CA 93721-1896

                                         Re: Your Request for Advice
                                             Our File No. A-84-283

Dear Ms. Montoy:

     Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of Fresno
City Councilmember Karen Humphrey concerning the recent
amendments to Government Code Section 84305. Effective
January 1, 1985, the law will require that where the sender of a
mass mailing is a controlled committee, the name of the person
controlling the committee shall be identified on the mailing in
addition to the name, street address and city of the committee.
You asked, if the name of the controlled committee (i.e.,
Friends of Karen Humphrey) already includes the name of the
person controlling the committee (Karen Humphrey), whether each
piece of mail must include both the name of the person and the
name of the committee (i.e., Karen Humphrey and Friends of Karen
Humphrey). It is not necessary to list the name of the person
controlling the committee separately on the mailing if the
person's name is in the name of the committee. The intent of
the amendment to the law is to require the disclosure of the
name of the controlling person when it is not otherwise
disclosed in the name of the committee.

     If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                         Sincerely,


                                         Diane Maura Fishburn
                                         Staff Counsel
                                         Legal Division

DMF:pla

City of

FRESNO

James A. McKelvey
City Attorney

November 6, 1984


Barbara Milman, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: AB 3435

Councilmember Karen Humphrey from the City of Fresno has asked
this office to make an inquiry relating to AB 3435 which amends
the Political Reform Act and becomes effective on January 1,
1985. This bill requires that where the sender of mass mailing
is a controlled committee the name of the person controlling
the committee shall be included on the outside of each piece of
mail in the mass mailing in addition to the name, street
address and city of the committee. The question raised is
whether this is required where the name of the controlled
committee already includes the name of the person controlling
the committee. For example, if Ms. Humphrey is a candidate for
the upcoming city election and she controls a committee named
"Friends of Karen Humphrey" will each piece of mail have to
include both "Karen Humphrey" and "Friends of Karen Humphrey"?

If you need clarification on this inquiry please let me know.
Thank you for your response.

Hilda Cantu Montoy
Hilda Cantu Montoy
Deputy City Attorney

HCM/zzd
6573/16

cc: Karen Humphrey, Councilmember


City Hall • Fresno, California 33721-1896 • 209 488-1326

sections.question: If the name of the controlled committee (i.e., Friends of Karen Humphrey) already includes the name of the person controlling the committee (Karen Humphrey), whether each piece of mail must include both the name of the person and the name of the committee (i.e., Karen Humphrey and Friends of Karen Humphrey).
sections.conclusion: It is not necessary to list the name of the person controlling the committee separately on the mailing if the person's name is in the name of the committee. The intent of the amendment to the law is to require the disclosure of the name of the controlling person when it is not otherwise disclosed in the name of the committee.
sections.question_synthetic: Under the amended Government Code Section 84305, must mass mailings sent by a controlled committee separately identify the controlling person's name if that person's name is already included in the committee's name?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No separate identification is required. If the controlling person's name is already included in the committee's name (e.g., 'Friends of Karen Humphrey'), listing it separately on the mailing is not necessary, as the amendment's intent is only to require disclosure when the controlling person's name is not otherwise apparent.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (from City Attorney) and FPPC's response letter. Both sections clearly present the question and conclusion. Minor OCR errors noted ('Scaff' for 'Staff', 'SACR..A.MENTO') but do not affect extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: If the name of the controlled committee (i.e., Friends of Karen Humphrey) already includes the name of the person controlling the committee (Karen Humphrey), whether each piece of mail must include both the name of the person and the name of the committee (i.e., Karen Humphrey and Friends of Karen Humphrey).

CONCLUSION: It is not necessary to list the name of the person controlling the committee separately on the mailing if the person's name is in the name of the committee. The intent of the amendment to the law is to require the disclosure of the name of the controlling person when it is not otherwise disclosed in the name of the committee.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that mass mailings by a controlled committee need not separately identify the controlling person's name if it is already included in the committee's name, pursuant to amended Government Code Section 84305 effective January 1, 1985.
